title: chrysothamnus parryi var. bolanderi
intro: Chrysothamnus parryi var. bolanderi, also known as Bolander's chamisa or Bolander's rabbitbrush, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Chrysothamnus parryi var. bolanderi have been linked to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing, providing health benefits such as relief from arthritis, wounds, and infections. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
